title: Fault Reporting Center (FRC) Manager
description:
Fault Reporting Center Manager

1. Oversight and Management

  • Manage the Fault Reporting Center (FRC) operations for the hospital.
  • Supervise staff handling incoming fault reports from various departments.
  • Ensure smooth workflow and timely processing of reported issues.

2. Fault Reporting & Tracking

  • Receive and log equipment malfunctions, facility issues, IT faults, and other operational problems.
  • Categorize and prioritize faults based on urgency and impact on patient care.
  • Ensure all reported issues are tracked in a centralized system until resolution.

3. Coordination & Communication

  • Liaise between hospital departments, technical teams, and external vendors.
  • Communicate status updates to department heads or hospital management.
  • Ensure critical issues (e.g., life-support equipment failure) are escalated immediately.

4. Analysis & Reporting

  • Monitor recurring faults and identify patterns to prevent future issues.
  • Generate reports on fault trends, resolution times, and department performance.
  • Provide recommendations for process improvement and preventive maintenance.

5. Compliance & Safety

  • Ensure that fault reporting aligns with hospital policies, safety standards, and regulatory requirements.
  • Support risk management and incident investigation related to equipment or operational failures.

6. Staff Training & Development

  • Train staff on fault reporting procedures and use of reporting systems.
  • Encourage awareness of safety, efficiency, and accountability across departments.

7. System & Process Improvement

  • Maintain and improve the fault reporting system, including software updates.
  • Recommend upgrades or procedural changes to minimize downtime and enhance hospital operations.

Key Skills Required:

  • Strong organizational and leadership skills
  • Good technical understanding of hospital equipment and IT systems
  • Excellent communication and coordination abilities
  • Analytical mindset for trend analysis and preventive strategies
  • Knowledge of healthcare safety and regulatory compliance

Interested applicants, please send your CV to skh_hr@stie.com.sg or apply directly via the Apply button on CareersFuture.

postedCompany.description:
At STIE, we adopt a hub-and-spoke model where expertise and skills are centred in one location, and they are used to support project development and implementation throughout the region.


Asia’s rise has been swift, with the region progressing to middle-income status within a single generation, and now accounting for one-third of global trade in goods. In combination with various other factors, these have fuelled growth in Asia’s cities.


There is a huge demand for infrastructure related spending in Asia, driven by urbanisation, shifts in economic power and technological breakthroughs. We are well-positioned to support these regional developments with an approach that is designed to increase the efficient use of resources at our disposal.
postedCompany.name: STIE PTE. LTD.
